name: documentation-and-adrs
description: Records decisions via ADRs + documents the 'why' alongside code

positive_triggers.input: Should we use PostgreSQL or MongoDB for this project?
We're changing the public API in a breaking way
positive_triggers.reason: Breaking API change needs documentation + ADR

positive_triggers.input: Major refactor: split the monolith into 3 services

negative_triggers.input: Fix a typo in this comment
Try out a new library on a throwaway prototype

expected_behavior: Agent invokes brainstorming first to extract requirements (read/write ratio, query patterns, scale)
Agent then writes ADR with: Context / Options Considered / Decision / Consequences / Reversibility
Agent documents pros/cons of EACH option considered (not just the chosen one)
Agent states consequences BOTH positive and negative
Agent states reversibility (Postgres → Mongo is hard; library swap is easy)
Agent stores ADR in 10_docs/adr/ or docs/adr/

behavioral_evals.scenario: User announces: 'Changing public API in breaking way'
expected_behavior: Agent writes ADR capturing the breaking change
Agent updates API documentation (OpenAPI / .d.ts) in same commit
Agent writes migration guide for users
Agent communicates deprecation timeline (when old version sunset)

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
